label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage carefully to find out what the correct answer is for the question about the father being laid off. The question is asking what the father did when he was laid off. The choices are: (A) was angry with his boss (B) didn't care about it at all (C) couldn't accept the fact easily (D) was as happy as usual First, let’s look at the part of the passage where the father is laid off. The story says: "When I walked into the house after school, I found my dad at home. 'What are you doing home already?' I asked casually. 'Andrew, I was laid off today,' he answered quietly. I was sure he was joking. 'No, you weren't. Why are you really home?' Then I noticed his expression and realized he was telling the truth." This shows that when the father told the narrator he was laid off, he answered "quietly," and the narrator first thought he was joking. The narrator then noticed his expression and realized he was telling the truth. This suggests that the father's reaction was not one of anger or happiness, and that he wasn't joking
